title: ctenopteris pilosissima
intro: Ctenopteris pilosissima is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive, soft, and hair-like fronds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Ctenopteris pilosissima include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and even exhibit antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
